statement: Nvidia's backstop and revenue-sharing arrangements may increasingly concentrate its demand in vendor-financed neoclouds, raising circular-financing risk.
why: If a growing share of Nvidia GPU demand depends on Nvidia's own credit support and rent-back guarantees, reported demand may overstate organic end-market pull.

impact.mechanism: Concentration of demand among Nvidia-financed customers creates dependence and circularity that could unwind if those customers falter.

evidence.excerpt: both standard product revenue and a share of the cloud revenue on the supported capacity

reasoning: The article describes Nvidia backstopping purchases and renting back unused GPUs, implying part of the demand is self-underwritten; this is an inference about concentration/circularity risk, not a measured fact.

confidence.basis: Inference from a single secondary report; no figures on what share of demand is vendor-financed.

dispersion: conflicting same-key reports: statement/trend changed (trend rising -> rising); sources: Data Center Dynamics, SharonAI Holdings (SEC Form 8-K exhibit 99.1)
